narrative.headline: 2020 Subaru Impreza: windshield and visibility lead the complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 84 complaints with NHTSA. Visibility/Wiper dominates at 62%, far ahead of everything else, with another 5% filed under Visibility. No deaths are reported across any area. The smaller complaint groups are where the few serious outcomes show up: brakes and air bags each have 1 crash, and air bags account for the only fire (1) and the only injuries (2).

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: By far the biggest complaint: 52 reports (62%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports point to windshield issues, so inspect the glass closely.

issues.sample: WHO: DAUGHTER 16YO (DRIVER), FATHER (FRONT PASSENGER SEAT), DAUGHTER 14YO (DRIVER SIDE REAR SEAT).    VEHICLE: 2020 SUBARU IMPREZA    WHAT: SUBARU ANTI-COLLISION ENGAGED UNNECESSARILY    WHEN: 0704 EST 14 MAY 2021    WHERE: 41°43'07.4"N 81°14'08.3"    MY 16YO DAUGHTER WAS DRIVING
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 5 reports (6%), including 1 crash and no injuries. A small but worth checking the brakes on a test drive.

issues.title: Visibility
issues.blurb: 4 reports (5%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. These echo the windshield theme seen in the top complaint.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 3 reports (4%) but the most serious by outcome: 1 crash, 1 fire, and 2 injuries. Watch for any air bag warning light.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 3 reports (4%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports describe the collision-avoidance system engaging when it should not.
